subject: Computer Networking
title: Ethernet Cables Explained
description: A micro-lesson that makes Ethernet Cables Explained usable.

hook.text: Cat5, Cat5e, Cat6, Cat6a, Cat8 — they look the same but perform very differently.

buildup.text: Ethernet cables use twisted copper pairs. Higher categories have tighter twists and better shielding.

discovery.text: Cat5e handles 1 Gbps over 100m. Cat6a reaches 10 Gbps. Cat8 hits 25-40 Gbps but only over 30m.

twist.text: The RJ45 connector is the same on all categories. The difference is inside the cable, not at the plug.

climax.text: For most homes, Cat6 is the sweet spot: 10 Gbps capable, affordable, and available in any length.

punchline.text: The cable category sets your speed ceiling. Choose wisely.

quiz.question: What speed can Cat6a Ethernet cable support?
